channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: Ebola outbreak in DR Congo: Over 80 dead, 246 cases reported in Ituri province; health emergency declared
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Meningitis outbreak sparks alarm after student dies and 2 others fall ill in Reading Area
item.description: Health authorities are investigating a meningitis outbreak in the Reading and Oxfordshire area after one student died and two others were hospitalized. The cases appear linked through social groups of young people, prompting concern and precautionary measures. Experts urge vigilance for symptoms, especially in teenagers and young adults.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: ‘We are devastated’: Father shares pain of losing ‘kind-hearted’ son to meningitis in England
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: BBC’s 72-year Commonwealth Games era ends as Channel 5 secures Glasgow 2026 highlights deal
item.description: For the first time in 72 years, the BBC will not broadcast the Commonwealth Games. Channel 5 will air daily highlights of the Glasgow 2026 event, following TNT Sports securing live rights. This shift reflects the BBC's financial pressures and a strategic pivot towards digital content, while Channel 5 expands its sports offerings.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: After Donald Trump, Putin to visit China's Xi Jinping on May 19 for two days
item.description: Russian President Putin is set for a significant two-day visit to China, commencing May 19, to deepen strategic ties with President Xi Jinping. This follows closely on the heels of Donald Trump's high-profile trip to Beijing, which focused on Taiwan, trade, and Iran. The leaders will discuss strengthening their partnership and address key global issues, aiming to sign a joint declaration.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Op Epic Fury to resume? Trump weighs fresh Iran strikes after China visit: Report
item.description: As US President Trump returns from China, pressure mounts over Iran. Top aides are reportedly preparing options for renewed military action against Tehran if diplomacy falters, including intensified bombing and potential Special Operations deployments. Iran, meanwhile, signals readiness for conflict, while the Strait of Hormuz remains a critical concern for global oil prices.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: After Trump’s Beijing visit, Taiwan detects Chinese naval vessels near its waters
item.description: Taiwan's defense ministry reported eight Chinese naval vessels near the island following US President Trump's China visit. Trump suggested China might act against Taiwan after his term, acknowledging Beijing's strong objections to its independence. The US arms package for Taiwan remains under review amid escalating tensions and China's territorial claims.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Lil Tjay sparks backlash after exposing private messages linked to Kai Cenat’s ex-girlfriend
item.description: Lil Tjay sparked online backlash after briefly posting and deleting screenshots of private messages allegedly involving Kai Cenat’s ex-girlfriend, Gigi Alayah. Fans criticised the rapper for exposing personal conversations, while others defended his actions amid ongoing tension with Cenat. The streamer has not publicly responded, and the controversy has already reignited debates about clout-chasing, celebrity privacy, and social media drama in entertainment culture.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz: I wouldn’t advise my kids to live or study in US
item.description: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz advised his children against studying or working in the US due to a rapidly changing social climate and job market difficulties for the highly educated. Merz expressed strong optimism about Germany's opportunities for young people, contrasting it with his concerns about the current US situation.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: What happened between Benny Johnson and Marjorie Taylor Greene? YouTuber faces grifting accusations during Thomas Massie controversy
item.description: Benny Johnson and Marjorie Taylor Greene became involved in a major online feud after Greene criticized Johnson’s YouTube coverage of hush-money allegations against Thomas Massie. The fight quickly turned personal, with both accusing each other of dishonesty and propaganda. Meanwhile, Massie strongly denied the accusations, calling them false and politically motivated just before his Kentucky primary election.
item.dc:creator: Sehjal Gupta

item.title: "Erika wanted his position": Candace Owens reacts after Charlie Kirk reportedly named wife Erika as TPUSA successor before assassination
item.description: Candace Owens has questioned claims that Charlie Kirk chose wife Erika Kirk as his successor at Turning Point USA before his assassination. Owens cast doubt on the authenticity of an audio recording allegedly captured during a private Aspen donor retreat and demanded the release of full video footage. TPUSA has refused, citing donor privacy concerns, further fueling online debate and controversy.
item.dc:creator: Barsha Dutta

item.title: Snakes everywhere: 120 Burmese python eggs discovered in Florida’s Everglades
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Super-rich NRI who called himself 'recession-proof' in 2008 sent to jail: His New Jersey mansion had salt water pool surrounded by imported sand
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Meet Zhou Qunfei: Once she worked in a glass factory, now she runs a $26 billion company and sits alongside Elon Musk and Tim Cook at China’s state dinner
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Chud the Builder visibly disappointed as bond hits $1.25 million
item.description: Popular streamer Chud the Builder, real name Dalton Eatherly, appeared visibly upset in court after a judge set his bond at $1.25 million. He faces serious charges including attempted murder following a shooting outside a Tennessee courthouse. Eatherly claims he acted in self-defense, but authorities are investigating the incident as a violent crime.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: 'No evidence you suffered in India': Inside Sanaullah Khan Mohammed's failed attempt to get asylum in US
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists discover ‘shy plant’ that can count without even having a brain
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: 15-year-old Canadian boy built a robotic turtle that can detect underwater environmental threats with 96% accuracy
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: A plant that “does math”: Scientists uncover hidden Voronoi patterns inside Chinese money plant leaves
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: 10 countries with highest anaconda population in the world: From Brazil to French Guiana
